findings.title: validate_logic duplicated verbatim across 5 Pydantic models — identical bodies will drift independently
findings.suggested_fix: Extract a module-level helper (e.g. in backend/validators.py): VALID_LOGIC = frozenset({"AND", "OR"}) and def validate_logic_value(v, allow_none=False): ... Import and call it from each model field_validator. Four of the five bodies are byte-for-byte identical; the fifth (UpdateBotRequest) handles Optional[str] — the helper should accept an allow_none flag. This makes the allowed values a single source of truth and prevents one model from silently accepting a third value if the set ever expands. Affected: backend/models.py:95, backend/bot_manager.py:56, backend/routes/bots.py:76, backend/routes/backtest_quick.py:42, backend/routes/backtest_quick.py:82.

findings.title: last_tick resolution IIFE duplicated three times in BotCard.tsx — lastTickStr defined but not reused at lines 170 and 337
findings.suggested_fix: The expression detail?.state.last_tick ?? summary.last_tick appears at line 44 inside heartbeatColor and is re-derived at lines 127, 170, and 337. Hoist to component scope as const lastTick = detail?.state.last_tick ?? summary.last_tick, then derive lastTickStr once and reference it for all three title attributes. This removes three independent readings of the same nullable chain.

findings.title: ChartSkeleton defined in App.tsx with a single call site — chart loading state belongs in chart feature directory
findings.suggested_fix: ChartSkeleton is a pure presentational component related to chart loading state. App.tsx is the orchestration hub — chart-specific UI does not belong here. Move to frontend/src/features/chart/ChartSkeleton.tsx and import it. Low urgency since the component is small, but App.tsx is already large and this establishes the right boundary.

residual_risks: The _ext (extended_hours) field added to the cache key tuple in shared.py means pre-existing in-process cache entries with the old 5-tuple shape will miss (not crash) on first access after deploy — correct behavior, no residual risk from the cache_info fix itself.

The validate_logic duplication (P2) means if a third logic operator is added in the future, it must be added to 5 separate validator bodies. Any missed update will produce inconsistent validation across endpoints silently at runtime — one endpoint accepting the new value while another rejects it.
testing_gaps: No test coverage for the validate_logic validators — a unit test asserting that each model rejects invalid values (e.g. NAND) would immediately surface drift between the five copies.
The NaN guard in OptimizerPanel.tsx (C25) has no automated test — a blank string for min/max is the trigger path; this is user-controlled input with no validation at the API level.
